frameworkNotes: 2023 Integrated Report + TCFD Climate Notebook (climate appendix). Pre-CSRD; CSR Department oversees climate reporting. Emissions figures disclosed are 2022 data (reporting lag). SBTi-certified well-below 2°C since February 2023.

assurance.scopeNote: Avoided-emissions indicator audited for the first time in 2022. Other indicator assurance not explicitly detailed in extracted section.

scope1.methodNote: Energy generation (28 Mt) + gas network methane emissions (2 Mt) + vehicle fleet and other (0.2 Mt). Operational control boundary.

scope2.methodNote: Purchased electricity and heat consumed by ENGIE for own use.

scope3.note: ENGIE combines this with Cat 1 under 'Procurement & fixed assets' (8 Mt total).
Upstream chain of fuel & electricity (extraction and transport of raw materials).
End consumption of gas volumes sold by ENGIE to end users.
Investments (incl. energy generation of equities not controlled by ENGIE).

targets.name: Carbon footprint of use of sold products 2030
Carbon intensity of energy generation and consumption

internalCarbonPrice.perimeterDescription: Internal CO2 price integrated into financial valuation of investment projects; based on internal scenarios of market decarbonization. Exact price level not surfaced in extracted section.
internalCarbonPrice.pricingMethodNote: CO2 MTP (Medium-Term Plan) assigns CO2 budgets to GBUs; infra-annual management via quarterly GHG indicators and QBRs.

risks.description: Heatwaves, droughts, floods, extreme winds across ENGIE assets. Studied at country-by-country level using IPCC RCP 2.6 / 4.5 / 8.5 scenarios. Partnership with Institut Pierre Simon Laplace for modeling.
2022 drought in France and Portugal: hydropower buybacks of 1.3 TWh for €127M EBIT impact.
2021 Texas cold wave: -€30M to -€40M net income loss.
2020-2050 under RCP 8.5: hydropower production -15% to +18% variation; solar -3% to +5%; wind -2% to +7%; thermal stable.
Cooling demand +13% to +109% by 2050 under RCP 8.5; heating demand -20% to -13%.

SBTi Power Sector guidance requires more ambitious 1.5°C-aligned trajectory than well-below 2°C ENGIE is certified for — gap disclosed. 1.5°C alignment would require Scope 1+2 reduction increase from -66% to -78% by 2030, requiring sale of current thermal plants.

climateCommittees: Ethics, Environment and Sustainable Development Committee
governance.transitionPlanReference: 2023 Integrated Report - Climate Notebook section + TCFD Addendum

transitionPlanSummary: Net Zero Carbon by 2045 across Scopes 1/2/3 via well-below 2°C SBTi-certified trajectory. Four operational levers: (1) worldwide coal phase-out by 2027, (2) massive renewable electricity investment (€22-25bn growth CapEx next 3 years, 75% EU Taxonomy aligned), (3) reduction and greening of gas volumes with biomethane and green hydrogen, (4) energy storage for grid flexibility. 99% of carbon footprint covered by decarbonization objective.
actionSummary: Sustainable finance: ~€18bn green bonds issued since 2014; CO2-performance-linked syndicated credit lines. Compensation: CEO variable pay 10% linked to CO2 emission targets; performance shares 10% CO2 + 5% renewables mix; 5,000 employees on long-term incentives with climate criteria. Sustainability Academy + Renewables Academy employee training. Research partnerships: CEA (FR), NREL (US), EnergyVille (BE). Top-250 supplier SBT alignment target 100% by 2030 (23% in 2022).
